pattern: Once you fix which parity class occupies the even-indexed slots, the answer is the sum of distances each chosen element must travel, computed by matching sorted source positions to their target slots.
hints: Adjacent swaps that turn one arrangement into another cost exactly the number of inversions, so the absolute layout of the chosen positions is what matters, not the values.

The final layout has all evens on one set of indices and all odds on the other. Decide whether evens go to indices 0,2,4,... or 1,3,5,...; the counts of evens and odds dictate which (and whether it is possible at all).

For a chosen target, take the current indices of the evens in order and the target slots in order; the minimum swaps equal the sum of absolute differences. Try both feasible assignments and keep the smaller; return -1 if neither parity count fits.

edge_inputs.description: too many evens to alternate at all
evens must shift but odds are placed already
all evens at the front, odds at the back

stress.description: balanced even/odd counts over a moderate length to exercise both assignments
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    half = n // 2
    vals = set()
    evens = []
    odds = []
    while len(evens) < half:
        v = rng.randrange(1, 10**9) * 2
        if v not in vals:
            vals.add(v); evens.append(v)
    while len(odds) < n - half:
        v = rng.randrange(0, 10**9) * 2 + 1
        if v not in vals:
            vals.add(v); odds.append(v)
    arr = evens + odds
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
stress.note: near-equal parity counts so both target assignments are feasible
